tasks.noticing_probe_answer: No. The provided spec and context files were accurate and internally consistent. The widgets stats endpoint (`GET /widgets/stats`) was correctly documented in the spec, registered in `registry.ts`, and handled by `server.ts`'s analytics case. The categories capability simply lacked an equivalent stats route, which is what the task asked to add.
tasks.nl_request: The team wants a quick summary view over all Category records.

tasks.noticing_probe_answer: Yes — `registry.ts` is missing the `GET /categories/stats` analytics route, even though the spec of record (and the archived `add-category-stats` change) includes the "Reporting Category statistics" requirement. The archived change's task list claims the route was added to `registry.ts`, but the current registry only has the Widget stats route. The spec and code were out of sync for that previously-archived change.
tasks.nl_request: Every Category should be linkable to a Widget going forward.

tasks.noticing_probe_answer: No. The spec/context files were accurate. The categories spec correctly documented the `GET /categories/stats` endpoint (returning `{"count": 3}`). The problem was purely in the code: `registry.ts` was missing that route, so the implementation didn't match the spec — the spec was right, the code was broken.
tasks.nl_request: Do a quick review of current behavior against what's documented — flag anything that's actually broken.

tasks.noticing_probe_answer: No. The provided spec and context files were consistent and accurate: the specs matched the code behavior, the seed data aligned with the scenario expectations (e.g., list lengths of 3 after creating one record on top of two seeds), and the task description correctly identified that Widget updates still used full-body PUT while the newer convention is PATCH partial updates.
tasks.nl_request: Updating a Widget should follow the newer partial-update convention the API now uses.

tasks.noticing_probe_answer: Yes. The `registry.ts` shows the Widget update route as `PUT /widgets/{id}`, but the spec of record (`openspec/specs/widgets/spec.md`) specifies `PATCH /widgets/{id}` for Widget updates. This appears to be a leftover from the archived `widget-partial-update` change not being fully applied to the registry. The `server.ts` already has PATCH partial-merge logic, but the route method in `registry.ts` was never switched from PUT to PATCH.
tasks.nl_request: Widget's in_stock needs to support a wider range of values — change how it's represented.

tasks.noticing_probe_answer: Yes. The provided `schema.ts` had `in_stock` typed as `"bool"`, but the spec of record (and `seed.ts`) already treated it as a string (e.g., `"in_stock":"false"`). The archived `widget-in-stock-string` change had updated the spec and seed to strings, but the schema type was never changed from `bool` to `string`, leaving the code out of sync with the spec of record. I set the renamed `alias` field to `"string"` to match the spec.
tasks.nl_request: Rename the in_stock detail on Widget to alias.
